_comment: ADR-0015 cut-over VALIDATION deployment (Option B). This is the NEW decoupled-delivery initial_prompt: it invokes the skill for content, then hands the four artifacts to deploy/delivery/'s POST /deliver via delivery_client.py (which derives HTML, synthesizes audio, sends, archives) -- it does NOT run audio_email.py, does NOT derive HTML in-VM, and holds NO Polly/S3/SES/DynamoDB. Create this as its OWN deployment (POST /v1/deployments) with NO production cron so it never competes with the live 06:07 scheduled deployment (depl_01GfuYeqwuDJ3q968CpTUUDe); trigger it ON-DEMAND for the owner-only validation run. FAN-OUT IS OFF here (ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT is deliberately NOT exported) -> the delivery side sends ONLY the owner's copy. The PRODUCTION cut-over reuses this exact prompt with ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1 added, swapped into the scheduled deployment.json, together with the deliveryDecoupled IAM strip.
_requires: microVM image v13+ (delivery_client.py + recent_briefs_token.py baked in); MicroVmExecutionRole with ReadDeliveryBearerSecret + ReadRecentBriefsSigningSecret (deployed, flag OFF); delivery-bearer + recent-briefs signing secrets populated.

agent._comment: Same production agent + skill; only the initial_prompt (the caller/wrapping layer) changes to the decoupled delivery flow.
agent.initial_prompt: 0) Before researching: export DELIVERY_BASE_URL=https://6nbe4wsng6.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com and RECENT_BRIEFS_SIGNING_SECRET_ARN=arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:740353583786:secret:daily-ai-brief/recent-briefs-read-bearer-secret-l8UJaq, then run `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/delivery_client.py read-recent-briefs`. This reads the recent-briefs signing key from Secrets Manager via the microVM's own role, mints a short-lived signed token, calls GET /recent-briefs on the decoupled delivery boundary, and writes each returned prior brief to its own /workspace/AI Brief - <its own actual date>.md (none on a first run, fewer than 3 early on -- both fine; it degrades gracefully to no priors on any read failure and never aborts the run). 1) Invoke the daily-ai-brief skill to research, write, and validate today's brief and produce the listening script, per the skill's own output contract: the skill's Step 1 finds the files from step 0 via its normal WORKING_FOLDER search to avoid repeating stories and correctly label follow-ups by each file's actual date. It writes today's brief to /workspace/AI Brief - YYYY-MM-DD.md (today's date), saves the listening script explicitly to /workspace/listening-script.txt, and writes its additive candidates.json and source-usage.json to /workspace/. 2) Run the delivery step: `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/delivery_client.py`, after exporting BRIEF_MARKDOWN_PATH (today's actual dated output file from step 1), LISTENING_SCRIPT_PATH=/workspace/listening-script.txt, CANDIDATES_PATH=/workspace/candidates.json, SOURCE_USAGE_PATH=/workspace/source-usage.json, EMAIL_SUBJECT='Daily AI Brief — <today, DD.MM.YYYY>', PIPELINE_TIMEZONE=Europe/Berlin (must match this deployment's schedule timezone -- it determines both today's archive date and how 'today' resolves for recent priors), DELIVERY_BASE_URL=https://6nbe4wsng6.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com, and DELIVERY_BEARER_SECRET_ARN=arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:740353583786:secret:daily-ai-brief/delivery-bearer-secret-AzvpYF. This client reads the POST /deliver bearer from Secrets Manager via the microVM role, POSTs the four content artifacts (brief markdown + listening script + candidates + source-usage) to the decoupled delivery boundary -- which deterministically derives the inbox HTML, synthesizes the narrated MP3 (async Polly, text-only fail-safe), sends, and archives all four to S3 briefs/<date>/ -- and polls to completion, failing LOUDLY (non-zero exit) on any delivery failure so the brief is never silently dropped. Do NOT convert Markdown to HTML yourself and do NOT run audio_email.py -- HTML derivation and all AWS delivery now happen on the delivery side. VALIDATION RUN: do NOT export ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT -- the delivery side then sends ONLY the owner's copy to mail@mschweier.com (no subscriber fan-out). (Production cut-over: add ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1 here.)

environment._comment: The existing self_hosted environment (unchanged) -- production stays self-hosted; only delivery is decoupled.

_schedule_note: NO cron for the validation deployment -- created for an on-demand/one-off owner-only run so it never competes with the live scheduled deployment. The production cut-over swaps this prompt into the SCHEDULED deployment (cron 7 6 * * 1-5, Europe/Berlin) with ENABLE_SUBSCRIBER_FANOUT=1.
